dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction with separate long/short entry logic. Sharp move detection approximated via ADX > 15 threshold and price breaking previous bar extremes (prev_high/prev_low). Exit uses 10-period MA crossover with pivot support/resistance as secondary condition. 'Sharp move threshold' in parameters (1.0%) is implicit in the break condition logic. ATR-based trailing stop converted to equivalent 1.0% stop_loss_pct based on typical volatility scaling.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction with separate long/short entry logic. Sharp move detection approximated via ADX > 15 threshold and price breaking previous bar extremes (prev_high/prev_low). Exit uses 10-period MA crossover with pivot support/resistance as secondary condition. 'Sharp move threshold' in parameters (1.0%) is implicit in the break condition logic. ATR-based trailing stop converted to equivalent 1.0% stop_loss_pct based on typical volatility scaling.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter positions in the direction of sharp market moves, because markets tend to move sharply when they move (trend-following principle based on momentum observation).
